reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — HAR runnability + correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (a licensed marathon photo -> Running 0.98, a person holding a cup -> Drinking 0.96, a street market -> Cycling 0.85), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (six licensed photos each classified with top-k bars). Gallery images are the ledgered licensed media/assets photos, shown directly with image-credit attribution.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, q8) BEFORE building. On distinct licensed photos the model reads the ACTIVITY: marathon -> Running 0.98, woman holding a cup -> Drinking 0.96, street market -> Cycling 0.85, plus Laughing/Dancing on ambiguous stills. Deployed page (scripts/validate-human-action.mjs 8/8): six photos classified with real softmax probabilities and top-k bars. The label tracks what's happening in the image, not a canned answer.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a module Worker via the transformers.js image-classification pipeline's low-level processor->model path (so we also get raw logits for the bars).

rubric.evidence: Single RGB image -> resized 224x224 (ViT patch-16) -> logits [15] -> softmax over 15 actions (Running, Cycling, Drinking, Eating, Dancing, Using Laptop, ...). A DISTINCT capability: it names the ACTIVITY, vs the built ImageNet object classifiers (vit/resnet/convnext/beit/mobilenet), and vs the food, document-type, facial-expression, age, nsfw and AI-image classifiers - none of which read what a person is doing.
rubric.notes: It's a single-still classifier, so it infers action from pose/context, not motion - honest about that in the copy.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (Human-Action-Recognition-ViT-Base, linked), task/pipeline (image-classification via transformers.js), the full 15-action label set, input (224x224 ViT), output (softmax over 15), quantisation (q8 model_quantized.onnx), download (~87 MB) and license (Apache-2.0, fine-tune of Apache-2.0 google/vit-base). No invented API.
rubric.notes: Uses the shared lib/webai.js loadPipeline + the canonical transformers.js image-classification pattern (same as the built food/ViT demos), so it composes with the download-inventory gate as transformers-pipeline.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/Human-Action-Recognition-VIT-Base-patch16-224-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:87}). transformers.js caches the model files, so lib/model-cache.js sees the cached model-id path -> a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~87 MB). Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and classified the gallery.
rubric.notes: Standard transformers.js runtime-owned caching (whole-file), adopted via the shared loader + <model-download-status>.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-classifies the six-photo gallery sequentially) -> classify-your-own via upload/drag-drop with a busy guard; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch surfaced in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> gallery -> (upload path wired) end-to-end.
rubric.notes: PENDING (recorded): a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder and a webcam path (classify a live frame).

rubric.evidence: A drag-and-drop + click file upload (accept=image/*) with a preview, plus the read-only classified gallery. Uploading classifies the user's own photo and renders top-k bars + timing.
rubric.notes: A webcam 'what am I doing?' path is a natural extension; recorded as a follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Every bar is the model's real softmax probability; the top action is just argmax. Ambiguous stills (a street scene, a portrait) honestly get lower-confidence, split guesses - the bars show it. Gallery images are the ledgered licensed media/assets photos shown directly with image-credit attribution (no new committed images).
rubric.notes: Honest about the single-still limitation: with no motion, near-actions (e.g. sitting vs listening-music) can be confused, and the bars make that visible rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: Status is role=status aria-live=polite; the dropzone is a focusable role=button with Enter/Space activation and a focus outline; gallery images have descriptive alt; each bar row is text (action name + percentage), so results aren't colour-only.
rubric.notes: The probability bars are decorative; the action name + percentage carry the information.

rubric.evidence: The gallery is flex-wrap; cards are fixed 200px and wrap; the upload row wraps. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-human-action.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Index verified desktop+mobile; subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a ViT-Base q8 forward pass on WASM is well under a second per image, so the six-photo gallery classifies quickly and uploads are near-instant. The ~87 MB q8 download is a one-time cost.
rubric.notes: Gallery photos are classified sequentially with a status line; no per-frame loop.

guidanceConsulted.query: run a transformers.js image classifier off the main thread; keep the UI responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); don't block the UI on model work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — classification runs in a module Worker via the transformers.js pipeline; the gallery classifies sequentially with a status line and uploads have a busy guard.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: page responsive during classification; sub-second per image

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive image-card gallery that wraps without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing; cap card widths; let rows wrap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .har-gallery is flex-wrap; cards are fixed 200px; the upload row wraps.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file dropzone + live-updating result region announced to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Make custom drop targets focusable role=button with keyboard activation; announce changing results via aria-live; give images descriptive alt.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dropzone is role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space + focus outline; status is aria-live; gallery images have alt.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: keyboard opens the picker; status announces the predicted action + timing

openQuestions: Would a short webcam clip + temporal smoothing meaningfully beat single-still classification for the motion-heavy actions (running vs cycling vs dancing)?
Are the 15 HAR classes broad enough to be useful, or does the demo need to be honest that it's a fixed, small action vocabulary?
followUpGoals: Add a webcam path ('what am I doing?') that classifies a live frame with temporal smoothing.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (clear single-action photos; multi-person / ambiguous scenes; motion-dependent actions where a still is genuinely hard).
Show the object-vs-action contrast side-by-side with the built ImageNet ViT on the same photo.
summary: Human action recognition (ViT-Base) — a distinct image-classification capability (WHAT a person is doing across 15 actions), separate from the built object/food/document/expression/age classifiers, proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM (marathon -> Running 0.98, cup -> Drinking 0.96). ~87 MB q8. Index demo complete + verified 8/8; webcam + subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
